fields.description:
## Why
Audit Reliability finding #2 (HIGH). In `src/rebar/_store/push.py`, `push_tickets_branch` performs a fetch + non-fast-forward `git merge` + stash mutation on the push-retry path that runs **unlocked**, by documented design (push runs after the write lock is released). The same fetch+merge on the sync path (`src/rebar/_store/sync.py` `reconverge` / `_do_reconverge`) is correctly guarded by the write lock. A concurrent writer that already passed its rebase guard can have its `git commit` fail against transient MERGE_HEAD/index activity from the push-retry merge, spuriously failing the user's write. In `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=async` mode this is routine: a detached background process merges while the foreground batch keeps writing. Related (audit 3.2): the async detached spawn in `push_tickets_branch` swallows its own `OSError` (`except OSError: pass`).

## What / files
- `src/rebar/_store/push.py` — in `push_tickets_branch`'s non-fast-forward retry/merge block: keep the `git fetch` OUTSIDE the lock (it only moves remote-tracking refs), then wrap the merge/stash mutation in `with _lock.write_lock(base_path, timeout=<short>, attempts=1, dual_window=True):`, mirroring `sync.py::reconverge` (fetch outside, `with write_lock(...): _do_reconverge(...)`). **Re-check `_lock.check_no_rebase_in_progress(base_path)` INSIDE the locked critical section** (as `_do_reconverge` does) — not only before acquiring the lock — to close the TOCTOU where a concurrent reconverge/push could create MERGE_HEAD between a pre-lock check and lock entry. On `_lock.LockTimeout`, skip the merge and leave the push pending (best-effort) rather than racing; never fail the user's write.
- Log the async-push detached-spawn `OSError` at WARNING (via the module logger) instead of `except OSError: pass`.

## Scope
In: `push.py` (lock around the merge portion with an inner rebase re-check; spawn-failure logging); one concurrency regression test. Out: `sync.py` (already correct); changing push best-effort semantics; stash-drop hardening (separate low finding).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The non-fast-forward reconcile merge in `push_tickets_branch` runs under `_lock.write_lock`, with `check_no_rebase_in_progress` re-checked INSIDE the lock; a lock timeout means the merge is skipped (push stays pending), verified by a regression test using the `two_clones` fixture in `tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py`
- [ ] The async-push detached-spawn failure path logs at WARNING — verified by a unit test that forces the spawn (`subprocess.Popen`) to raise `OSError` and asserts a WARNING record via `caplog` (no silent `except OSError: pass`)
- [ ] New test: a concurrent locked write during a push-retry merge — the write succeeds (no spurious StoreError) and no event is lost
- [ ] Push remains best-effort: no code path lets a push failure fail a write (existing push/sync tests stay green)
- [ ] `make test` green
